one_sentence_thesis: Negative feedback can move a policy beyond the Positive-only target, but historical reuse can turn remote negatives into excessive aggregate influence; DRPO retains the original curse-of-repulsion research identity while selectively attenuating the far-field component of that same negative term.
lineage_statement: The manuscript is the theoretical reconstruction and generalization of the original DRPO paper (arXiv:2602.10430), not a newly named unrelated algorithm.
strategy_driven_optimizations: Separate slow-moving cross-paper writing principles from the evolving DRPO-specific manuscript strategy.
Record the original Optimistic-DRO/hard-filtering paper as the explicit lineage for the retained DRPO name.
Remove live experiment state, seed queues, and execution steps from the structural outline.
Remove historical Product-manifold provenance from the main environment table.
Consolidate six experiment RQs into four paper-level questions.
Elevate the empirical aggregate negative term to a mandatory theory-method-experiment observable.
Preserve matched badness-distance or quality-rarity isolation as the decisive rival-explanation control.

gates.evidence: The active strategy and v0.9.1 outline share one concise useful-to-destructive repulsion thesis.
All six Introduction moves, Theorem 1, DRPO, four RQs, and Figure 1 advance the same central tension.
Novelty is the missing bridge from matched badness-distance isolation to causal transmission, equilibrium transition, and selective control.
Unrelated global-guarantee and fixed-field disclaimers are absent; claim calibration is handled through controls and status.
Theorem 1 has one job: characterize Positive-only, stable extrapolation, boundary approach, and loss of finite equilibrium.
Theory names q m_-, DRPO produces q_lambda m_{-,lambda}, and RQ3 measures an empirical M^- proxy.
The four RQs specify controls, observables, fairness, terminal evidence, and environment responsibilities; live statuses remain in handoff/registry.
C-U1 and D-U1 are controlled environments; Hopper/Countdown are external; Product manifold is appendix provenance only.
The main rival explanation—far samples are merely worse—is matched through context, quality, |A|, count, coefficient, and same-ray/Jacobian closure.
Paired seeds, matched or measured budgets, best/terminal reporting, and terminal audit are mandatory.
Task-performance collapse, support/variance/probability boundary events, and NaN/Inf are reported separately.
No unfinished external number is inserted; scientific status remains governed by handoff and registry.
Figure 1 carries Positive-only, stable extrapolation, far-field boundary/drift, matched badness-distance logic, and DRPO recovery.
The six outline and blueprint blocks align in ID, title, order, and parent SHA-256; hierarchy points to v0.9.1/v0.5.

minor: C-U1 full-parameter distance-only attribution still requires the registered paper-facing same-state/same-ray radial probe or Jacobian-gain closure before prose may state the full claim.
D-U1 common/rare paper-facing isolation still requires the registered quality/semantics/count/base-coefficient matching audit.
Hopper and Countdown external-anchor and external-closure claims require formal terminal-audited results before quantitative prose.
Any steady-state method ranking remains governed by the registered terminal and confirmation audits in the handoff/registry.
The method chapter must retain a concise mathematical connection between the original Optimistic-DRO hard endpoint and the smooth exponential far-field envelope.

unresolved_experiment_dependencies.dependency: C-U1 same-state/same-ray radial probe or registered Jacobian-gain decomposition
formal Hopper and Countdown terminal-audited runs
